title: lastrea phegopteris
intro: Lastrea phegopteris, also known as the maidenhair spleenwort, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Athyriaceae. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various health conditions such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Lastrea phegopteris include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and its leaves can be dried and used in potpourri or as a garnish for salads.
